title: Father homesteading in Troy
description: His father took homestead by Troy in 1884.

title: Sly shoots Marshall Hays; Sly snuck into Moscow before he was lynched
description: Sly shoots Marshall Hays. Townspeople want to lynch Sly, and he is snuck out of town into Moscow as fast as the team could go. Hays was overbearing, but he was an Oddfellow and had friends among them.

title: Shoemaker stabs Driscoll in Troy; he gets paroled and leaves his wife
description: Shoemaker stabs Driscoll in Troy after they exchange remarks about a girl, and he dies because so many arteries are cut. Shoemaker is apprehended the next day. When he gets parolled, he tells his wife she has grey hairs and he leaves her. He told Jesse's mother that he liked to shoot Filipinos in the war to see them kick.

title: Clemm killed by a restaurant owner in Troy
description: Clemm was killed by a Black restaurant owner in Troy.

title: Family got along well; Joe's relation with the Wells brothers; father's slave
description: As a youngster, Jesse told black Joe Wells to wash his face, and it tickled Joe. Joe escapes from Hays in town. The family got along well with the whites. Joe's relation to the Wells brothers, slave of their father back east.

title: Homesteads on Moscow Mountain; fire on Moscow Mountain; naming sister Dolly
description: Mr. Stephenson homesteads on Moscow Mountain north of Troy in 1900. The place has reverted to timber, and there was a fire there. Mrs. Spencer cooked and took care of her family from the age of twelve. Naming her sister Dolly.

title: Cooking for the family; nursing father through scarlet fever; brother sick at age three; treating scarlet fever; home remedies
description: Cooking for the family: Mrs. Spencer started learning as a tot. Nursing her father through scarlet fever. Brother had to learn to walk again after getting sick at age of three. Treating scarlet fever. Home remedies for treating chest and throat flu.

title: Jesse's homesteading; raising fat cattle on bunchgrass; irrigated farming; rattlesnakes
description: Jesse's homesteading, twenty miles from Coulee City, 1907-1919. Raising fat cattle on bunchgrass; irrigated farming. Rattlesnakes stayed in rocky country. Pulling hides off cattle with a team.

title: Not liking milk or butter; working at the university
description: Jesse doesn't like milk or butter, because when he worked at the university they strained out the gargot and used the milk.

title: Giving up the homestead; threshing in Coulee country
description: Giving up the homestead, returning to farm 19 near Troy after they got married. Before they started threshing in the Coulee country, he drove her around to see the land. Doing an early morning wash, she was terrified by a bullsnake.

title: Working in the kitchen in Coulee country; skilled threshing operation
description: Working in the harvest kitchen in the Coulee country. Mrs. Spencer becomes first cook because the other cook is getting too old to do the work,and lightens her tasks. It was an extremely skilled threshing operation: every man had a job, and they only worked a ten hour day. If they did over 1300 sacks a day, the men got a chicken dinner.

title: Quality of springs in Coulee country; farming
description: The crew drank lemonade instead of the alkalai water. Quality of some springs in the Coulee Country. Farming not nearly as good as this part of Idaho. The one huge yellow pine on the place.

title: Grandmother homesteaded on Moscow Mountain; herding sheep; spinning wool
description: Grandmother Spencer homesteaded on Moscow Mountain, did all the work on the place except what the boys helped her with. She herded sheep and made spinning wool.

title: First meeting with the Spencers; Frank Rowan bought her a dinner basket
description: First time the Spencers met he pulled her hair, she got mad at him. Then she saw him again at his brother's birthday party in 1915, and asked Frank Rowan who the good looking fellow was. Frank buys her dinner basket from an old man at the schoolhouse social; the money was used to buy needed items for the school.

title: Achievements in school; walking two and a half miles to school
description: Jesse's achievements in school. Mrs. Spencer wanted to go to high school badly, but had to take care of her new baby brother, when she was 20. Walking two and a half miles to school.

title: Wild Davey's cabin
description: Wild Davey had a cabin near Park, and looked like a hippy.

title: Shipping wood from Troy; made living by cutting wood
description: Shipping wood from Troy; families made a living by cutting a hundred cords of wood a year.

title: Riprapping the railroad down the canyon
description: Italians were run out of Troy while they were riprapping the railroad down the canyon.

title: IWWs cleaned up camps; blamed for things they never did
description: IWWs cleaned up the camps, and were blamed for things they never did.

title: Robber of the Bank of Troy
description: Robber of Bank of Troy got caught because he was careless.

title: Sam McKeon and his daughter hiding moonshine
description: Sam McKeon and his daughter fool the law by hiding moonshine under blocks of wood they're sitting on. He hides moonshine in a pile of chips,

title: McKeon shot heel off Summerfield's boot; Mrs. McKeon threatens Gabriel Anderson
description: McKeon buries moonshine. It's said that McKeon shot heel off Summerfield's boot. Mrs. McKeon threatens Gabriel Anderson to discourage him from climbing the pole and disconnecting her phone.

title: Handling horses right; favorite teams; devising a hitch; training a lazy horse
description: Handling horses right: fitting collars and hames properly. A salve of axel grease and stove polish healed horses soreness. Favorite teams: one worked by themselves; one horse died from squirrel poison. Mrs. Spencer's father, who'd been a cowboy in California, thought Jesse was great with horses. How Jesse devised a hitch to even-up a three horse team so all pulled the same. He could even a big and a little horse, and a tender and a tough-mouthed horse. He could cut the hair off a horses back with a line. No good horseman uses a whip. Training a lazy horse without hollering at him. Balky horses caused by ill-fitting gear.

title: Driving 32 head of horses; heading; horses killed in runaways
description: Driving 32 head of horses on first combines by throwing rocks at them. Heading. Horses killed in runaways when hitches broke.

title: Hitching up a colt for the first time; training an outlaw horse while currying him
description: Jesse hitches up a colt for the first time with his mother on a snowy trip with their new baby, and Mrs. Spencer's father gets mad. Jesse trains an outlaw horse while currying him. The horse gets crazy and starts a runaway. A horse that kicked and would never give up.

title: Making cough syrup; treating tuberculosis; digging golf on Moscow Mountain
description: Making mullein cough syrup. Treating tuberculosis. Getting shivareed. Duthie dug gold on Moscow Mountain north of Troy, and fashioned gold charms out of the nuggets. More shivaree.

title: Problems with well and water; warm water wells; digging a well at the Troy homestead
description: Problems with well and water in the Big Bend country. Warm water wells. A pump at the bottom of a deep well is lost as it caves in, because the owners wouldn't take Jesse's advice. Digging well at the Troy homestead.

title: Father cutting timber; carried fifty pound sacks of flour from Moscow
description: Jesse's father made a living cutting timber. He cleared up an acre or two a year. Father carried fifty pound sacks of flour on his shoulder from Moscow. How their family got by in those days by being almost self-sufficient. The Spencers still grow produce as much as they can.

title: Making a living in the first years; growing their own food; managing money; necessities vs. luxuries
description: Making a living in the first years after they were married (1919), as a teamster, growing timothy, and growing own food. Man and wife have to pull together and manage their money carefully. Going in debt is what's the matter with the world. Difference between necessities and luxuries. A family that lives high above their means.

title: Training a reluctant horse to pull
description: Old Weyerhaeuser cries because he won't live to see all the timber cut. Jesse trains a reluctant horse to pull.

title: Wet fall of 1893 and loss of the crop; man talked about hard times under Cleveland; IWWs cleaned up camps
description: The wet fall of 1893 and the loss of the crop is what made the depression felt here. On a job during the thirties, a man talked about the hard times under Cleveland, but was told times had changed, that he owed his job to Roosevelt. IWWs cleaned up the camps, and were blamed for things they didn't do.
